title: Front-Running the Calm

mood.label: Heavy going 🌧️
mood.summary: A heavy-leaning mixed day: missiles over the Gulf and a ceasefire on the brink, Venezuela's quake toll near fifteen hundred and Ebola still burning in the Congo, while markets shrug and a handful of quiet wins — coral mapped, a cancer beaten back, an elk on the overpass — keep the day from going fully dark.

style.genre: marimba-led trip-hop / neo-soul — a sampled marimba tracing a circling topline over a slow, swung AkaiLinn kit with deep tape-echo, a sampled harp answering in the gaps, a plucked upright bass and a soft sine sub holding the floor under a low bowed-string pad; B melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh keeping the minor from sinking, a market staying oddly calm above a fragile reality

sampleSources: Strudel default library — VCSL marimba, harp & strings, AkaiLinn, sampled piano
pattern: stack( s("~ rim ~ rim").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.32).room(0.25).delay(0.2).delaytime(0.33).pan(0.4), s("bd ~ ~ [~ bd] ~ ~ bd ~").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.62).lpf(2400), s("~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~ sd ~").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.4).room(0.35).delay(0.25).delaytime(0.375), s("hh*8").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(sine.range(0.1,0.26).slow(7)).pan(0.6), note("<[b2,d3,fs3,gs3] [e3,gs3,b3] [fs3,as3,cs4] [b2,d3,fs3]>").s("piano").attack(0.02).release(1.4).gain(0.3).lpf(sine.range(900,2200).slow(12)).room(0.4), note("b4 ~ as4 fs4 ~ gs4 b4 ~ ~ fs4 e4 ~ gs4 ~ d4 ~").s("marimba").gain(0.5).room(0.3).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.375).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.18).pan(0.7)), note("~ ~ fs5 ~ ~ b5 ~ gs5 ~ ~ ~ as5 ~ ~ fs5 ~").s("harp").gain(0.26).room(0.45).rsize(3).pan(0.3).hpf(400), note("b1 ~ b1 fs2 ~ e2 ~ d2").s("piano").gain(0.34).lpf(700), note("<b1 fs1 e1 b1>").s("sine").gain(0.3).lpf(220), note("<b3 b3 e3 fs3>").s("sawtooth").attack(2).release(3).gain(0.14).lpf(600).room(0.6) )

lines: Sirens over Kuwait while the screens stay green,
oil four months low and the futures lean,
they're betting the strait reopens by dawn —
front-running calm with the ceasefire near gone.
One name in Doha the shrapnel found,
and a memorandum coming apart on the ground.
Caracas keeps counting, fifteen hundred near,
the rescue window closing, the dust still here;
the Congo holds a fever no vaccine can break,
and the heat dome in Europe won't give for our sake.
But somewhere a reef map tripled in size,
and an elk crossed six lanes under open skies.
Heavy going, but the light won't fold —
a cancer turned back, a young life told;
the market shrugs and the missiles fly,
we hold both at once under the same grey sky.
Front-running the calm, we count what's true:
the grief and the good both coming through.
